crime.value: Death of 6-year-old Pippa when medical equipment failed while mother was away
crime.quote: her BiPap stopped working and her Cannula came loose. And I couldn't help the poor 6-year-old girl.

victim.value: 6-year-old Pippa, medically dependent child left alone during routine errand
victim.quote: She died when I left her. Not because of murder. But because her BiPap stopped working

closed_world.value: Asylum with poor security where narrator is confined after claiming to be dead
closed_world.quote: The asylum I lived in wasn't very good at keeping people in, per se, more at keeping people out.

culprit_and_motive.value: Mother blames herself for leaving daughter alone, seeks death to reunite with her
culprit_and_motive.quote: But I killed my daughter, didn't I? That was when the horrible realization hit. I killed myself.

investigator.value: Mother Leah, unreliable narrator in asylum seeking supernatural reunion with dead daughter
investigator.quote: I wasn't ready for it when it happened. I thought I could handle him. I thought I could do it by myself.

red_herring.value: Initial suggestion that daughter was murdered by porcelain doll, when actual cause was medical equipment failure
red_herring.quote: She died when I left her. Not because of murder. But because her BiPap stopped working and her Cannula came loose.

clue_fairness.value: Medical equipment details (BiPap and Cannula) provided to reader before revelation of true cause of death
clue_fairness.quote: But because her BiPap stopped working and her Cannula came loose.

social_world.value: Institutional power of asylum system - guards, rules about contact, patient confinement and monitoring
social_world.quote: contact with the outside was barred. Phones were taken away once you entered.

alibi.value: Mother's absence during daughter's death - left to get groceries when medical emergency occurred
alibi.quote: I left her at home to get the freaking groceries. GROCERIES! She died when I left her.

reveal_mechanic.value: Psychological realization that narrator killed herself metaphorically through guilt over daughter's death
reveal_mechanic.quote: But I killed my daughter, didn't I? That was when the horrible realization hit. I killed myself.

media_and_audience.value: Institutional narrative that frames grief and guilt as insanity requiring asylum confinement
media_and_audience.quote: Now they think I'm insane. Funny isn't it? The moment they see you've had a little revelation

investigator_wound.value: Mother's overwhelming guilt and trauma from failing to prevent daughter's death while absent
investigator_wound.quote: I was a horrible mother. Horrible. And they say I went insane.
